title: Seviyan Vermicelli

seo.title: Seviyan Vermicelli Recipe: Easy Pakistani Dessert
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Pakistani Seviyan Vermicelli with our easy recipe. This sweet vermicelli dessert is perfect for any occasion, especially Eid. Get step-by-step instructions!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, sweet Pakistani Seviyan Vermicelli garnished with nuts and dried fruit.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, sweet flavors of traditional Pakistani Seviyan Vermicelli, a beloved dessert.

description: Seviyan Vermicelli is a beloved Pakistani sweet dish, often enjoyed during Eid and special occasions. This comforting dessert features roasted fine vermicelli cooked in milk, sugar, and aromatic spices like cardamom. Garnished with nuts and dried fruits, it offers a delightful blend of creamy texture and rich, sweet flavors that everyone loves.

instructions.title: Roast the Vermicelli
instructions.text: Heat ghee in a large, heavy-bottomed pan or pot over medium heat. Add the broken fine vermicelli and roast, stirring constantly, until it turns golden brown and releases a fragrant aroma. This usually takes about 5-7 minutes. Be careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Infuse the Milk
instructions.text: In a separate pot, bring the full-fat milk to a gentle boil. Add the lightly crushed green cardamom pods to the milk. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 2-3 minutes to allow the cardamom flavor to infuse into the milk.

instructions.title: Combine and Cook
instructions.text: Carefully add the roasted vermicelli to the simmering milk. Stir well to ensure all the vermicelli strands are submerged in the milk. Bring the mixture back to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Sweeten and Simmer
instructions.text: Add the granulated sugar to the vermicelli and milk mixture. Stir until the sugar is completely dissolved. Reduce the heat to very low, cover the pan, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes. The vermicelli should become tender and absorb most of the milk, resulting in a creamy consistency. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom.

instructions.title: Add Garnishes and Finish
instructions.text: Once the seviyan is tender and creamy, stir in the chopped almonds, pistachios, and raisins (if using). If you are using khoya, gently fold in the grated khoya for extra richness. For an aromatic touch, add rose water at this stage.

instructions.title: Serve Warm or Chilled
instructions.text: Remove the pan from the heat and let the Seviyan Vermicelli rest for a few minutes before serving. It can be enjoyed warm, straight from the pot, or chilled for a refreshing dessert. Garnish with additional nuts if desired before serving.

tips: Always roast the vermicelli on low to medium heat with constant stirring to ensure even browning and prevent burning, which can impart a bitter taste to your dessert.
For an extra rich and creamy texture, consider adding a tablespoon or two of condensed milk or grated khoya (reduced milk solids) along with the sugar.

Seviyan Vermicelli can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. When reheating, add a splash of milk and warm gently on the stovetop or in the microwave if it has thickened too much.
article: Seviyan Vermicelli is a beloved Pakistani Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
